source_quote: the other, a gibbet, with some chains hanging to it

name: Narrator recalls that the chains once held a pirate
source_quote: chains hanging to it which had once held a pirate

name: Unidentified man limps towards the gibbet
source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter
location: towards the gibbet on the edge of the river

name: Narrator perceives the man limping toward the gibbet
source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter
location: edge of the river, near the gibbet

name: Narrator imagines or interprets the man as a pirate come back to life and returning to the gibbet
source_quote: as if he were the pirate come to life, and come down, and going back to hook himself up again
location: edge of the river, near the gibbet

name: Narrator feels a terrible shock at his own thought
source_quote: It gave me a terrible turn when I thought so
location: edge of the river, near the gibbet

name: Narrator perceives cattle lifting their heads
source_quote: as I saw the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him

name: Cattle raise heads and gaze after the horrible young man
source_quote: the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him

name: Narrator wonders whether cattle share his frightening thought
source_quote: I wondered whether they thought so too

name: Narrator searches the surroundings for the horrible young man
source_quote: I looked all round for the horrible young man

name: Narrator fails to see any sign of the horrible young man
source_quote: and could see no signs of him

name: Narrator becomes frightened again
source_quote: But now I was frightened again

name: Narrator runs home without stopping
source_quote: and ran home without stopping
location: from the field area to home
